data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

THC Farmaceuticals ( (CBDG) ) has provided an announcement.

On December 31, 2024, the board of THC Farmaceuticals, Inc. approved a change to the company’s fiscal year end from September 30 to December 31 in order to align its reporting cycle with the calendar year and improve the comparability of its financial reporting and planning processes. The company will file the required transition reports for the short fiscal period created by this shift, and the change was implemented without amendments to its Articles of Incorporation or Bylaws, indicating an operational rather than structural adjustment that should streamline reporting for stakeholders and enhance alignment with standard market practice.

More about THC Farmaceuticals

THC Farmaceuticals, Inc. operates in the pharmaceutical sector, with a focus that includes regulated, disclosure-based financial reporting common to public companies in its industry.

data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Cullen/Frost Bankers ( (CFR) ) has issued an announcement.

On January 27, 2026, longtime director Dr. Chris Avery notified Cullen/Frost Bankers that he will retire and not stand for re-election when his term expires at the company’s 2026 annual shareholders’ meeting scheduled for April 29, 2026, marking the end of board service that began in 2015 and spanned major initiatives such as organic expansion and the COVID-19 pandemic. On January 28, 2026, the company’s board expanded its size from 13 to 15 members and immediately elected Marsha M. Shields, CEO and managing partner of McCombs Enterprises, and veteran Ernst & Young audit leader Jeffrey M. Rummel as independent directors serving on the Audit and Risk Committees, bolstering Cullen/Frost’s governance with deep Texas business ties and substantial financial, risk and corporate oversight experience that is expected to support the bank’s ongoing growth strategy.

The most recent analyst rating on (CFR) stock is a Hold with a $144.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Cullen/Frost Bankers stock, see the CFR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CFR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CFR is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by solid underlying financial performance and constructive earnings-call guidance (improving NIM and raised income outlook). Offsetting factors include asset-quality migration (problem loans and charge-offs) and expense growth pressure, while technical signals are neutral and valuation is moderately supportive.

To see Spark’s full report on CFR stock, click here.

More about Cullen/Frost Bankers

Cullen/Frost Bankers, Inc., through its Frost subsidiary, is a Texas-based financial holding company and one of the 50 largest U.S. banks by asset size, with $53 billion in assets as of December 31, 2025. Frost provides a full range of banking, investment and insurance services to businesses and individuals across major Texas markets including Austin, Corpus Christi, Dallas, Fort Worth, Houston, the Permian Basin, the Rio Grande Valley and San Antonio, continuing a franchise founded in 1868 and focused on serving Texans’ financial needs.

data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Spark I Acquisition Corp. Class A ( (SPKL) ) has shared an update.

On January 27, 2026, Spark I Acquisition Corp., a Nasdaq-listed special purpose acquisition company focused on consummating a business combination, disclosed that it had received a notice from Nasdaq stating the company was not in compliance with the exchange’s requirement to hold an annual meeting of shareholders within twelve months of the end of its fiscal year. The notice does not immediately affect the listing or trading of Spark I’s securities, but the company has until March 13, 2026, to submit a plan to regain compliance and could receive an extension until June 29, 2026, to hold the required annual meeting, with the possibility of an appeal if its plan is rejected, introducing a measure of regulatory uncertainty that shareholders and potential transaction counterparties will be monitoring closely.

More about Spark I Acquisition Corp. Class A

Spark I Acquisition Corp. is a special purpose acquisition company (SPAC) formed to effect a merger, share exchange, asset acquisition, share purchase, reorganization or similar business combination, with its securities listed on the Nasdaq Stock Market.

On 30 January 2026, Santander UK announced that Mahesh Aditya has been appointed Chief Executive Officer of Santander UK Group Holdings plc and Santander UK plc, with effect from 1 March 2026. Aditya joins from Banco Santander, where he has served as Group Chief Risk Officer since 2023 and previously held senior roles in the United States, including Chief Risk Officer for Santander US and CEO of Santander Consumer USA from 2019 to 2023, following more than three decades in financial services at firms such as Citi, JPMorgan Chase & Co and Capital One. His appointment, after joining the Santander UK boards in October 2025, signals a leadership transition that brings deep risk-management and international banking experience to the UK arm, potentially strengthening its governance and strategic alignment with the wider Santander group.

data.title: Source Capital Extends Discount Framework, Emphasizes Private Credit
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from Source Capital Inc. ( (SOR) ).

For the Discount Management Program measurement period from January 1, 2025 to December 31, 2025, Source Capital’s shares traded at an average discount to net asset value of less than 10%, moving to a premium in the fourth quarter and reaching a 1.20% premium on November 28, 2025; consequently, the contingent tender offer planned for calendar year 2025 will not be executed. The board extended the discount management framework through 2027, adding a new contingent tender offer for 10% of outstanding common shares at 98% of NAV if the average discount exceeds 10% in 2027, while also maintaining the stock repurchase program and signaling growing strategic emphasis on private credit, which accounted for 21.7% of NAV (25.9% including commitments) as of December 31, 2025, underscoring a shift toward illiquid credit exposures that could influence both returns and liquidity for shareholders.

More about Source Capital Inc.

Source Capital Inc. (NYSE: SOR) is a closed-end investment company managed by First Pacific Advisors, LP, targeting maximum total return through both capital appreciation and investment income while emphasizing capital preservation. Listed on the New York Stock Exchange, the fund can invest in longer-duration assets such as dividend-paying equities and illiquid private loans, positioning it for investors with a long-term horizon of at least five years.

data.title: Mega Fortune Delays Annual 20-F Yearly Report
data.description:
Mega Fortune Company Limited ( (MGRT) ) has released a notification of late filing.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Mega Fortune Company Limited has notified investors that it will be late in filing its annual Form 20-F (Yearly Report) for the fiscal year ended September 30, 2025, via a Form 12b-25. The delay relates specifically to its Form 20-F (Yearly Report), which will contain the audited financial statements for the year.

The company attributes the holdup to administrative delays in preparing the Form 20-F (Yearly Report) and, in particular, to a delay in obtaining the necessary consent from a prior auditor whose work must be included in the filing. There is no indication of internal control failures, a change of auditor, or a special committee review; the issue is presented as procedural and administrative rather than operational or financial.

Mega Fortune expects to file the delayed Form 20-F (Yearly Report) on or before the 15th calendar day after the original due date, in line with the relief provided under Form 12b-25. The company also confirms that all other required SEC periodic reports over the past 12 months have been filed on time, signaling an otherwise consistent compliance record.

Importantly for investors, the company states that it does not anticipate any significant change in its operating results compared with the prior fiscal year to be reflected in the upcoming earnings statements. However, the notification includes standard forward-looking statement language, cautioning that actual results and timing could differ due to various risks and uncertainties, including potential changes to anticipated financial results and the timing of auditor consent.

The filing underscores Mega Fortune’s intention to remain in compliance with SEC reporting requirements, using the permitted extension to finalize administrative matters. The notification is signed on behalf of the company by Chief Executive Officer Siu Fung Tang, dated January 30, 2026, signaling executive-level oversight of the delayed filing and the ongoing effort to complete the Form 20-F (Yearly Report) promptly.

More about Mega Fortune Company Limited

data.title: Protalix Wins EMA Panel Backing for New Elfabrio Dosing
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Protalix ( (PLX) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On January 30, 2026, Protalix BioTherapeutics and its partner Chiesi Global Rare Diseases announced that the European Medicines Agency’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use issued a positive opinion recommending approval of a 2 mg/kg every‑four‑weeks dosing regimen of Elfabrio for adult Fabry disease patients who are stable on enzyme replacement therapy. The opinion, based on long‑term clinical data including the BRIGHT study and its extension, will now be reviewed by the European Commission, and if approved is expected to significantly reduce treatment burden by extending infusion intervals, potentially improving quality of life for eligible patients while also positioning Protalix and Chiesi more strongly in the Fabry disease market and triggering a $25 million regulatory milestone payment to Protalix.

The most recent analyst rating on (PLX) stock is a Hold with a $2.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Protalix stock, see the PLX Stock Forecast page.

Earnings-call updates were supportive (strong YTD revenue growth, positive quarterly income, adequate near-term cash) while technicals are modestly positive and valuation remains somewhat demanding.

To see Spark’s full report on PLX stock, click here.

More about Protalix

data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

Dominion Energy ( (D) ) just unveiled an update.

On January 30, 2026, Dominion Energy reported updated cost and timing guidance for its Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind (CVOW) project after a temporary suspension of work stemming from a December 2025 order from the U.S. Department of the Interior’s Bureau of Ocean Energy Management and the impact of tariffs. The estimated total project cost, including contingency but excluding financing, has risen from about $11.2 billion to approximately $11.5 billion, and full project completion is now expected in early 2027, though first power delivery remains targeted for the first quarter of 2026. Dominion said the project is roughly 71% complete, fully permitted at the state and federal levels, and positioned as a key, bipartisan-backed component of Virginia’s energy strategy, delivering nearly 3 GW to support AI and cyber demand, shipbuilding and military installations, while creating about 2,000 U.S. jobs and $2 billion in economic activity, which underscores both its strategic importance and the cost and schedule risks inherent in large offshore wind developments.

The most recent analyst rating on (D) stock is a Hold with a $69.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Dominion Energy stock, see the D Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on D Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, D is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held back by pressured profitability and significantly negative free cash flow, despite improved leverage. Technicals are supportive with the stock trading above major moving averages and a positive MACD, while earnings commentary and recent legal developments around CVOW are net-positive but tempered by cost increases and execution risks. Valuation is balanced by a strong dividend yield alongside a middling P/E.

Company and Filing Details:
New Century Logistics (BVI) Limited has filed a Form 12b-25 notifying investors of a delay in submitting its Form 20-F (Yearly Report) for the fiscal year ended September 30, 2025.

Reason for Delay:
The company says it needs more time to review and finalize its financial statements before it can complete the Form 20-F (Yearly Report). The delay is described as procedural, tied to the internal review process, rather than to any specific external shock, auditor change or identified control failure.

Expected Filing Timeline:
New Century Logistics (BVI) Limited expects to file its Form 20-F (Yearly Report) within the standard 15-calendar-day extension period allowed under SEC Rule 12b-25. The company explicitly states it anticipates meeting this extended deadline.

Financial Impact and Statements:
Management indicates it does not expect any significant change in results of operations compared with the prior fiscal year to be reflected in the upcoming annual earnings statements. No preliminary figures are provided, and any expectations about future filing timing and financial results should be viewed as forward-looking and therefore subject to change if new information emerges during the completion of the audit and reporting process.

Next Steps and Signature:
The company notes that all other required SEC periodic reports over the past 12 months have been filed, signaling continued efforts to remain in compliance despite this short delay. The notification is signed on behalf of New Century Logistics (BVI) Limited by Chief Executive Officer Ching Shun Ngan, dated January 30, 2026, underscoring management’s responsibility for and commitment to completing the Form 20-F (Yearly Report) within the extended timeframe.

Shareholders overwhelmingly approved an extension amendment proposal, with 7,644,519 votes in favor, 1,000 against and no abstentions, while the adjournment proposal was not needed. In connection with the vote, holders of 17,773 Class A ordinary shares elected to redeem their shares for cash at about $13.39 per share, for an aggregate redemption of roughly $238,039. Following these redemptions and an initial $5,000 deposit into the trust account, the trust balance stands at about $628,176, and the company now has 7,646,529 Class A ordinary shares outstanding, of which 46,529 are held by public shareholders.

Director Ned Holmes has trimmed his stake in Prosperity Bancshares, selling 1,100 shares of the company’s stock in a transaction valued at $73,341.

Recent Updates on PB stock

Over the last 24 hours, trading in Prosperity Bancshares has been dominated by reaction to its newly announced agreement to acquire Stellar Bancorp in a mixed cash-and-stock deal that expands Prosperity’s Houston presence and brings in a high‑quality deposit base while deploying excess capital. Although the strategic logic and long‑term growth potential of the merger are broadly acknowledged, multiple analysts have become more cautious on the near‑term outlook. Piper Sandler’s reduced stance and lower price objective stem from concerns that the price paid for Stellar makes the five‑year tangible book value earn‑back period relatively long, which they believe could limit near‑term share outperformance as investors weigh integration risks and one‑time costs against the strategic benefits. Barclays also trimmed its target, emphasizing that the Stellar acquisition “steals the spotlight” from an otherwise solid Q4 earnings print and that the longer expected earn‑back profile has pressured the stock. Meanwhile, quantitative and AI‑driven assessments remain constructive, citing Prosperity’s strong financial position, profitability, and attractive valuation alongside favorable net interest margin and M&A‑driven growth prospects, but they still flag rising non‑performing assets and integration headwinds as factors tempering upside. Overall, the stock’s recent movement reflects this tug‑of‑war between enthusiasm for the expanded franchise and caution over the cost, payback period, and execution risk of integrating multiple deals at once.

Spark’s Take on PB Stock

PB scores well on valuation and earnings-call outlook (improving NIM guidance, capital returns, and strategic expansion), supported by solid underlying profitability and a stable balance sheet. Recent Updates on PKBK stock

Parke Bancorp’s recent news centered on management commentary from President and CEO Vito Pantilione, who characterized 2025 as a challenging year amid significant macroeconomic and political shifts. He highlighted that the second Trump administration implemented aggressive policy changes—such as new tariffs, expanded gas drilling, stricter border protection, and renewed efforts to curb illegal immigration—alongside rising tensions with the Federal Reserve over the pace of interest-rate cuts. These conditions suggest a more uncertain operating environment for regional banks like Parke, as shifting trade and energy policies and unclear monetary policy timing can affect loan demand, credit quality, and funding costs. The company also continued its regular capital-return policy with a declared quarterly dividend, signaling management’s confidence in underlying financial stability despite the difficult macro backdrop. Price target revisions over the last 24 hours would therefore likely be driven less by company-specific surprises and more by analysts recalibrating expectations for earnings and balance-sheet resilience under these evolving policy and rate conditions, particularly how prolonged uncertainty around rate cuts may pressure net interest margins and growth assumptions.
